title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Program Brief: Pure Core Shell
summary: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pure core shell increment in testable command lifecycle and function map. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-pure-core-shell — Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_cli_lifecycle_contract_4(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Pure Core Shell model · Theory Pure Core Shell, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Pure Core Shell precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Pure Core Shell model · Theory Pure Core Shell, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 9257 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Pure Core Shell model · Theory Pure Core Shell, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Pure functions cannot participate in a real command-line program.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-pure-core-shell-132erf.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-pure-core-shell-132erf(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_pure_core_shell_132erf\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not import_safe)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?side_effect_boundaries > 3)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?hidden_globals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status != 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
